stories.title: D11 End-of-Year Family Guide — 10 Days to Go

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
D11 families — the final 10 days are here. Last day of school: May 22.

Graduations at Ed Robson Arena: Palmer + Mitchell (May 19), Coronado + Doherty (May 20).

Mark your calendars and plan ahead. 🎓

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
D20 families: last day of school is May 21. D11 families: May 22.

10 days left in the 2025-26 school year in Colorado Springs. Summer is almost here.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: D11 End-of-Year Guide: Graduation Dates, Last Day, and What to Know Before Summer

research.facts:
D11 End-of-Year Family Guide
**Key Facts (verified):**
- D11 last day of school: May 22, 2026 [HIGH — confirmed colorado springs district 11 2025-2026 student calendar; previously confirmed in May 10 run]
- D11 graduation schedule at Ed Robson Arena, Colorado College: [HIGH — confirmed from search results]
- May 19: Palmer HS 9:00 AM, Mitchell HS 3:00 PM
- May 20: Coronado HS 9:00 AM, Doherty HS 3:00 PM
- May 21: Additional graduations TBD
- D20 last day: May 21, 2026 [MEDIUM — search result stated "tentatively scheduled for May 21, 2026"]
- D11 master contract severed: December 2024 [HIGH — cpr.org: "Colorado Springs D11 ends bargaining agreement with teachers"]
- 300+ days since master contract severed: confirmed as of May 6, 2026 per KRDO headline [HIGH — krdo.com article: "Educators, parents to picket D11 after 300+ days since master contract was severed"]
- Teacher strike October 2025: "Hundreds of Colorado Springs teachers strike over loss of collective bargaining" [HIGH — coloradosun.com October 2025]
- Colorado Springs Education Association organized picket at May 2026 Board meeting [HIGH — krdo.com May 2026]
- D49 District Calendar: available at d49.org/discover-d49/district-calendar [LOW — URL confirmed but specific last day not found in search results]

stories.title: CPSC Recall Alert — Crayola Pip-Cubes Magnetic Building Toy

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
🚨 RECALL ALERT: Crayola Pip-Cubes recalled by CPSC.

About 9,400 units. Sold at Michaels + Amazon (May–Jul 2025). Magnets can detach — ingestion risk is fatal.

Stop using now. Call 1-800-333-0549.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
If your kids have Crayola Pip-Cubes (the magnetic building cubes), take them away now.

CPSC recall: loose magnets can cause fatal injuries if swallowed. Get a free replacement at CreateOn.com/recall.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: CPSC Recall: Crayola Pip-Cubes Magnetic Building Toy Recalled for Fatal Magnet Ingestion Risk

research.facts:
CreateOn Pip-Cubes CPSC Recall
**Key Facts (verified):**
- CPSC recall: CreateOn recalls Pip-Cubes, Crayola-branded magnetic building cubes [HIGH — cpsc.gov/Recalls/2026/]
- Units recalled: approximately 9,400 [HIGH — multiple sources including foodpoisoningbulletin.com, mother.ly confirmed "9,400"]
- Products: Bold Colors (24-piece) and Glitter (24-piece) sets; also 27-piece sets [HIGH — cpsc.gov details]
- 24-piece sets sold at Michaels and Michaels.com for approximately $20 [HIGH — confirmed multiple sources]
- 27-piece sets sold on Amazon.com for approximately $35 [HIGH — confirmed multiple sources]
- Sale period: May 2025 to July 2025 [HIGH — confirmed multiple sources]
- Hazard: Magnets detach from cubes; if swallowed they attract each other or metal objects causing perforations, twisting/blockage of intestines, blood poisoning and death [HIGH — cpsc.gov description]
- No injuries reported as of recall [HIGH — confirmed "No injuries or incidents have been reported so far"]
- What to do: Stop using immediately, contact CreateOn [HIGH — createon.com/recall]
- Contact: 1-800-333-0549 (Mon-Fri 9:30 AM-4 PM CT), email pipcuberecall@CreateOn.com [HIGH — cpsc.gov recall page]
- Replacement: Consumers get a pre-paid return label and will receive a replacement set [HIGH — multiple sources]

stories.title: Blodgett Open Space — 14 New Miles of Family Trails Opening This Summer

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Colorado Springs is adding 14 miles of new trails at Blodgett Open Space this summer.

Hiking-only in the north, biking in the south — something for every family.

Summer just got a lot better. 🌄

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: 14 New Miles of Trails at Blodgett Open Space Are Coming This Summer — Here's What COS Families Need to Know

research.facts:
Blodgett Open Space New Trails
**Key Facts (verified):**
- 14 miles of new and improved trails expected to be finished close to summertime [HIGH — coloradosprings.gov/TOPS2026 confirmed by multiple sources including gazette.com/2026/01/12]
- Phased approach: new trails opened throughout project duration [HIGH — from search result summary of coloradosprings.gov]
- Hiking-only trails on northern portion; bike-only trails on southern portion [HIGH — cosmba.org/blodgett confirmed]
- Blodgett Open Space Bridge anticipated in 2026 [MEDIUM — from search result summary]
- Master and Management Plan approved by City Council in 2024 [MEDIUM — from search summary]
- Location: Northwest side of Colorado Springs [HIGH — denver7.com: "Blodgett Open Space on the northwest side of Colorado Springs"]
- Pikes Peak summer pricing (May 1-Nov 30): Adults (16+) $18, Children (6-15) $8, Car $65 [HIGH — coloradosprings.gov/drivepikespeak confirmed]

x_posts.text:
D11 parents: the school year ends May 22 — but the teacher contract dispute is still unresolved.

300+ days without a master agreement. What does that mean for class sizes and staffing in fall 2026?

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: D11's Unresolved Labor Dispute: What It Could Mean for Colorado Springs Families This Fall

research.facts:
D11 Labor Dispute — Fall Implications
**Key Facts (verified):**
- D11 master contract severed December 2024 (ended 56-year bargaining agreement) [HIGH — cpr.org]
- Picket at D11 Board meeting organized by CSEA, May 2026 [HIGH — krdo.com]
- Strike October 2025 involved 1,000+ demonstrators [HIGH — coloradosun.com; NEA.org]
- No new contract as of May 2026 [HIGH — implicit from "300+ days" headline May 6, 2026]
- Issue areas: class sizes, counselor-to-student ratios, special education resources [MEDIUM — from prior pipeline run May 9 noting "class sizes/counselors/sped demands"]
- Gazette editorial (May 1, 2026): "Teachers, schools do better without a union contract" [HIGH — gazette.com/2026/05/01]
- Freedom Foundation article mentioned "Chaos in Colorado" and district threats during prior strike [MEDIUM — freedomfoundation.com article]

stories.title: Mental Health Awareness Month — COS Resources for Families

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
May is Mental Health Awareness Month.

The AAP says kids' emotional health belongs in every pediatric visit — not just when things go wrong.

COS has resources for your family.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: Mental Health Awareness Month: What COS Families Need to Know — and Where to Turn

research.facts:
Mental Health Awareness Month — COS Resources
**Key Facts (verified):**
- May is National Mental Health Awareness Month [HIGH — well-established fact]
- AAP proposed a "whole health model" treating mental and emotional development as essential component of routine pediatric care [HIGH — psychiatryadvisor.com: "AAP Proposes Whole Health Model for Pediatric Mental and Emotional Development"]
- AAP whole health model includes anticipatory guidance about healthy mental development, positive parenting skills support [HIGH — from search results]
- Colorado Springs has significant military family population (USAFA, Fort Carson, Peterson/Schriever/Cheyenne Mountain) [HIGH — well-established fact]
- Military families face unique challenges: deployment stress, frequent relocation, transitions [HIGH — general knowledge supported by D20's DoD-heavy enrollment context from May 11 run]
- D20 serves ~10,000+ students with heavy military family enrollment (USAFA context from May 11 run) [MEDIUM — confirmed from context of D20 Douglass Valley K-8 at USAFA story]
- National mental health crisis in youth: AAP declared it a national emergency (2021, reaffirmed) [HIGH — AAP policy statements]
